Introduction 
Correct and timely diagnosis is essential for effective treatments and improving patient outcomes. Diagnosis is a multidisciplinary endeavor involving clinical decision-making between multiple clinicians in coordination with patients, families, and caregivers, involving laboratory, pathology, and radiological testing, all within a complex healthcare system. Failure to make an accurate and timely diagnosis is common and can be harmful, costly, and even fatal. The National Academies of Sciences, Engineering,
